Title: Emergence of a proton exchange-based isomerization and lactonization mechanism in the plant coumarin synthase COSY

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the first committed step and directs hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA intermediates toward coumarin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced prior characterization and phylogenetic analysis in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtF6′H1 catalyzes ortho-hydroxylation of feruloyl-CoA, creating the branch point into coumarin biosynthesis. 2. Phylogenetic analysis suggests F6′H emerged in early eudicots and is absent in monocots. 3. Its emergence postdates COSY, implying COSY was recruited into coumarin biosynthesis later.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important pathway context gene but not a primary mechanistic subject in Results.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key upstream partner to stack with COSY for engineering enhanced coumarin production.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrates for COSY assays by converting free acids to CoA thioesters
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme use in coupled assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4CL1 was used to generate 6OHFCoA and 2OHpCCoA substrates in coupled assays. 2. It enabled biochemical testing of COSY and ortholog activities. 3. It functions as an upstream substrate-supplying enzyme rather than the focal mechanistic gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assay-support gene, not a primary research target for nutrient metabolite causality in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling enzyme for pathway reconstitution with COSY in synthetic biology.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron acquisition from soil; plant defense against herbivores and pathogens; regulation of plant–microbiome interactions; pharmacological activities including antiviral, antibacterial, and anticoagulant effects
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Amplified from root cDNA; in planta role supported in roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Accelerates trans-to-cis isomerization and intramolecular lactonization of o-hydroxylated trans-hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrates to form coumarins
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream step after F6′H-mediated ortho-hydroxylation; terminal coumarin-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Lowers the activation energy of the rate-limiting trans-to-cis isomerization step in coumarin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coumarin formation, especially scopoletin and sideretin accumulation; cosy mutants show reduced scopoletin/sideretin and iron-deficiency growth phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structures; site-directed mutagenesis; coupled enzyme assay with At4CL1; LC-MS metabolite profiling; deuterium exchange assays in vitro and in planta; molecular dynamics; QM/DFT modeling; mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCOSY is the central enzyme studied and accelerates coumarin formation from o-hydroxylated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrates. 2. Structural and mutational analyses show an unconventional BAHD active site with occluded acyl-acceptor pocket adapted for single-substrate intramolecular acyl transfer. 3. Deuterium-exchange, MD, and QM data support a proton exchange-based mechanism at C2 that lowers the barrier of the rate-limiting trans-to-cis isomerization before lactonization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: cosy mutant plants display iron-deficiency growth phenotypes under iron-deficient soil due to reduced coumarin output
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light during coupled assay; D2O labeling conditions; iron-deficient soil for mutant phenotype context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conserved BAHD His161 is dispensable for overall activity, unlike canonical BAHD enzymes. Product-bound and CoA-bound structures deposited as 8DQO, 8DQP, 8DQQ, 8DQR. Active-site residues Trp371, Gly166, and Leu374 contribute to proton exchange/catalysis. The enzyme likely predates widespread coumarin biosynthesis in angiosperms.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance coumarin production for improved iron acquisition and specialized metabolite engineering; likely most effective when stacked with upstream coumarin-pathway genes such as F6′H1 to ensure precursor supply; caution that coumarin pathway deployment may alter rhizosphere interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic analysis using Phytozome v12.1.6; LC-HRAM-MS raw data deposited in Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.7513211

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes coumarin formation from o-hydroxylated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows coumarin synthase activity comparable to AtCOSY in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein production and in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmCOSY is a COSY ortholog from Glycine max. 2. Recombinant GmCOSY catalyzes scopoletin formation in vitro with activity comparable to AtCOSY. 3. Supports conservation of COSY biochemical function in eudicots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly for ortholog functional comparison; not the mechanistic focus of the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential donor for coumarin engineering in heterologous systems if species-specific deployment is desired.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: AlphaFold2 model provided in Source data

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes coumarin formation from o-hydroxylated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows coumarin synthase activity comparable to AtCOSY in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein production and in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StCOSY is a COSY ortholog from Solanum tuberosum. 2. Recombinant StCOSY catalyzes scopoletin formation in vitro with activity comparable to AtCOSY. 3. Supports conservation of COSY biochemical function across eudicot species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential alternative ortholog for coumarin metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: AlphaFold2 model provided in Source data

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Putative ancestral COSY-related activity; tested in coumarin biosynthesis assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes coumarin formation from o-hydroxylated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA substrate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal coumarin-forming step in assay context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows coumarin synthase activity comparable to AtCOSY in vitro despite monocot origin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein production and in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCOSY from Sorghum bicolor shows coumarin synthase activity in vitro comparable to AtCOSY. 2. This supports that COSY-like catalytic potential predates the emergence of F6′H and widespread eudicot coumarin biosynthesis. 3. Suggests possible ancestral biochemical activity of COSY orthologs in monocots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes that coumarins are not widely reported in monocots, so in vivo function of SbCOSY remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could serve as a biochemically active ortholog for heterologous engineering or comparative evolution studies.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: AlphaFold2 model provided in Source data
